string.text: Anyone else feel like hit squads are moving along with them? Same familiar faces and rifles keep showing up.
From the Garbage to Agroprom the same group was on my tail. Different faces, same patches. Feels like there’s a price on my head.
Twice today I heard radio chatter cut off the moment I went on air. Don’t believe that’s just a coincidence.
Every time I change my route, someone’s already watching that road. Feels like my PDA is being read before I even touch it.
Whoever’s paying to “just scare me a little” is wasting money. These guys stopped at “just scare” a long time ago.
Am I the only one whose “easy stashes” from anonymous messages always end with someone looking down iron sights at their back?
Three times in a week I changed camps, and the tracks around my sleeping bag are still fresh. Someone’s walking in my footsteps.
Heard talk in the bar about a “valuable target” described a bit too much like me. Guess my dossier is already circling the Zone.
Anyone know how to wipe bounty data from PDAs? Asking for a man who doesn’t want to get his throat cut in his sleep.
Traders suddenly know way too much about where I’ve been and what I brought back. Either the walls have ears or some hunter talks too much.
Heard my description on the radio twice already, from different voices. That’s more than just campfire gossip.
In every camp they look at me like they’re quietly counting how much my head goes for these days.
Relax, stalker. Bounty hunters don’t waste time on every passerby. If they came for you, someone had their reasons.
The Zone already twists your mind enough. Sleep, eat, and then check whether those “tails” of yours are still there.
Most “hunter squads” are just hungry rookies in the same jackets. Don’t turn them into legends too early.
If they really wanted you gone, you wouldn’t have time to whine about it in the public channel.
Not every rumor at the bar is a contract on your head. The Zone eats the weak much faster than the mercs do.
Bounties come and go. Walk quietly for a day and there’ll be another lucky guy on the board.
Not every squad behind you is after you. Sometimes everyone just happens to take the same road.
Tracks around a camp can belong to anyone – mutants, stalkers, or even you, running in circles in a panic.
If your name really was on the list, you’d hear the whispers at the bar before the voices in your head.
Those who keep looking over their shoulder stop noticing the real danger. Watch your step, not just your back.
Stick closer to friendly bases for a while. Hunters hate witnesses almost as much as they love easy prey.
Don’t walk alone, move with a group. Even psychos count guns before they jump into a fight.
If you’re sure they’re on you, change your armor, your usual paths and the time you head out. Make them work for their money.
Hit the bar and talk to the barman. If the bounty is real, the rumor will reach him long before it reaches you.
Hide your stash somewhere even you don’t want to go often. Hunters don’t like going far with no guaranteed payoff.
Pick the worse roads, away from open paths. Hunters like clear fields, not swamps and anomaly clusters.
If you’ve got friends with “weight”, now’s the time to remind them you’re still alive and plan to stay that way.
Keep radio chatter to a minimum. Names, routes and loot size – that’s what good contracts are made from.
Calm down, hero. Hunters go after fat prey, not someone who barely crawled in from the Garbage.
If there really is a bounty on you, the client overpaid. That’s a bad investment.
Those “tracks around your camp” are probably just you, pacing in circles and whispering about hunters.
Sure those following you are mercs and not a pack of dogs that smelled fear and cheap booze?
Next time you see the “same guns”, check first if they’re not just hanging in your imagination.
The Zone has enough problems without you. You don’t have to play the main character in every campfire horror story.
Drop your coordinates if you’re so sure you’re being followed. We’ll all come over and check.
The only “bounty on your head” is in your head. The bulletin board sure hasn’t seen you.
Heh, send the coordinates of where they “chase you”. We’ll come with the whole squad and see if there are hunters or just your delusions.
If they’re really after you, why are you still alive? Maybe they just like you and are too shy to say hello.
Take a picture of these “hunters” and share it in the channel. Let’s see if they’re not just lost tourists.
How much are they paying for your head? Name a number, maybe I’ll come see if you’re worth it myself.
“They’re watching me, tracks everywhere, radios go silent!” — sounds like a bad movie script. Sleep it off.
Set up a meeting with those “hunters” at the 100 Rads Bar. We’ll come, laugh a bit and drink to your nerves.
You know what hunters really get paid for? Keeping quiet. And here you are chattering like a magpie. No one will pay that much for you.
Walk into an open field and wait an hour. If no one shows up, go get treatment. If they do, at least you’ll prove you’re not a paranoiac.
Your “pursuers” are probably tired of running in circles after you. Give them the coordinates so they can rest.
If you’re so valuable that a whole squad is chasing you, let’s meet up — I want to see a living legend.
Keep whining in the public channel and hunters won’t even need to be paid – someone will shut you up for free.
Voices that pop up too often on the radio usually don’t last long in the Zone. Keep it up.
If no one’s put a price on you yet, this thread is a good reason for someone to fix that mistake.
Keep screaming about how “they’re hunting you”. Eventually the ones who really know how to track will hear it.
Hunters love targets that broadcast their routes over the radio. You’ve already given them a head start.
So sure you’re under the gun? Take a slow walk alone. Someone will definitely want to test that theory.
The Zone doesn’t need “wanted” posters when someone makes too much noise on the air. Those problems get solved fast.
Speak up, stalker. When they finally come for you, everyone will want to hear how this story ends.
If you’re scared someone’s on your trail, we can arrange that. Drop your route and we’ll test your theory.
Keep yelling about hunters. We’ll remember your voice when we decide to check how right you were.
You want someone really on your tail? Keep whining on the air – people will get curious if you’ve got anything worth taking.
Drop the coordinates if you’re that scared. We’ll come and make sure those “hunters” aren’t just us.
Loud targets like you don’t live long. Not because they’re hunted, but because they never shut up in time.
You do realize that after talks like this someone will take an interest? Just out of curiosity — who’s that scared of dying.
Tell us where they “hunt” you and we’ll tell you where we’ll find you. Fair trade of information, isn’t it?
